title: toxicodendron longipes
intro: Toxicodendron longipes, commonly known as the poison sumac, is a plant species native to the southeastern United States, characterized by its glossy leaves and clusters of small greenish-white flowers. The plant contains urushiol, a toxic compound that causes skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals. However, when used in moderation and under proper guidance, the plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and made into teas, tinctures, or poultices to harness its health benefits, such as treating skin conditions, reducing fever, and alleviating pain.
